slides.title: Offline workload access for user-owned resources without refresh tokens
PEDIGREE: per-hop delegation above workload credentials
SOOS: Mandate JWT & Cross-Principal Transaction ID (XPID)

chat.author: John O'Leary
chat.text: Anyone else having issues with sound? I can't hear a thing and the speaker icon is greyed out. I can see the slides though.

chat.author: Pieter Kasselman
chat.text: @meetecho the slide clicker is not working

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Were the slides passed to the clicker?

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: I see Justin controlling them

chat.author: Pieter Kasselman
chat.text: yes

chat.author: Justin Richer
chat.text: yes I passed them to the clicker

chat.author: Justin Richer
chat.text: I took control back when the clicker didn't work

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: Is there alignment with WebBotAuth?

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: If response signing is optional does that mean signature stripping is an attack?

chat.author: Justin Richer
chat.text: clicker works now! thank you @meetecho

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Perfect!

chat.author: RAMPALLI Karthik
chat.text: I would like to contribute to both the WIMSE issues and the last call.

chat.author: Henk Birkholz
chat.text: Use hands tool next time

chat.author: Henk Birkholz
chat.text: Include remote attendees, pls

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: WebBotAuth doesn't have alignment with itself, does it?

chat.author: Aaron Parecki
chat.text: I am confused what alignment there would be, aren't they both just independent uses of HTTPSig?

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: it is about the new signature parameters introduce in between -02 and -05

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: Should those be in WebBotAuth?

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: different input parameters, different key resolution, different trust anchoring

chat.author: Aaron Parecki
chat.text: Plz don't do WGLC on all the drafts at once

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: so you are right @aaron but also there's lack of alignment in some respects

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: Aaron is right about that too

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: I need to digest the changes to the layered auth, because it's (cryptographically) non-trivial.

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: But I'm a little bit afraid of how the layers are bound together (or not) depending on which endpoints share cryptographic state (or not)

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: but AIFIK WebBotAuth hasn't decided on its approach (aka conceded what cloudflare has already done)

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: If there's a way to have them collaborate, it would be nice to have alignment where possible and then a clear understanding of where use cases diverge enough to require something else.

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: It's WebBotAuth though, I do get that.

chat.author: Aaron Parecki
chat.text: I thought from a use case perspective they were totally unrelated

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: still the audience and non signature parameters feel more best practices than WIMSE specifics

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: nonce*

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: i think, in a way, you are both (Kathleen and Aaron) right

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: @Aaron Parecki  They have a lot of overlap in certain contexts. Certainly a lot of the initial thinking was inspired by some of the same problems.

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: This talk for example seems like a huge overlap with WBA

chat.author: Pieter Kasselman
chat.text: WBA? WebBotAuth?

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: Yeah

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: the overlap is sounding more overlapping with this trust bundle 'problem space'

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: to Jonathan's point there

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: Which draft is this?

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-klrc-aiagent-auth/

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: ty!

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: Slides - https://datatracker.ietf.org/meeting/126/materials/slides-126-wimse-aims-00

chat.author: Brian Campbell
chat.text: bitte

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: So this would be interesting to see where WebBothAuth maps into the larger framework. Would likely help in industry. Good to hear they like SPIFFE

chat.author: Elaine Ly
chat.text: Could someone clarify whether WIMSE is meant to replace SPIFFE or supplement it, and how?

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: SPIFFE has been adopting WIMSE output, which is a good result

chat.author: Alissa Cooper
chat.text: Because identity in this draft is scoped to the wimse concept of trust domain, the framework here does not apply to consumer agents (like, an agent I run on my laptop to do things just for me), correct?

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: My understanding is that WIMSE is a superset of SPIFFE.

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: SPIFFE was underspecified and this work towards defining standards and ensuring interoperability has helped people work together and align more.

chat.author: Nathanael Ritz
chat.text: WIMSE is looking to create an open standard out of what SPIFFE started (I think)

chat.author: Thibault Meunier
chat.text: I would second Alissa comments. My understanding is ai agents might be workload, but not all of them are

chat.author: Nathanael Ritz
chat.text: "proposed" standard

chat.author: Flemming Andreasen
chat.text: One superset example: JWT-SVID is purely bearer-based. With WPT you now get proof of possession for app-level identity

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text:
ai agents might be workload

All ARE workload

chat.author: Thibault Meunier
chat.text: Is a Web browser a workload?

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: Does it consume compute? If yes it is a Workload

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: the ones with built in multimodal models might be

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text:
Alissa Cooper said:

Because identity in this draft is scoped to the wimse concept of trust domain, the framework here does not apply to consumer agents (like, an agent I run on my laptop to do things just for me), correct?

I don't see why a locally running agent wouldn't count as a workload? It just means the local machine is its own trust domain that other people can make their own trust decision on, no?

chat.author: RAMPALLI Karthik
chat.text: +1 Would be happy to contribute.

chat.author: Elaine Ly
chat.text: Does that imply that locally running agents can also use WIMSE http signatures and tokens?

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text:
Thibault Meunier said:

Is a Web browser a workload?

In a vacuous sense, sure. If you wanted to have your web browser authn + authz to e.g. a locally running model I don't see a logical reason why you couldn't. It might not be super useful, but from a logical perspective is there a reason why that shouldn't work?

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: If they can use a key that they can bound to their  credential, why not?

chat.author: Alissa Cooper
chat.text: I think what is considered a trust domain has significant implications when we say things like "every agent needs to be uniquely identified." If we are then talking about uniquely identifying multiple different pieces of software (or workloads) running on an individual user's machine, that has different privacy properties from the enterprise-deploys-a-workload case.

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text:
Elaine Ly said:

Does that imply that locally running agents can also use WIMSE http signatures and tokens?

For a single machine the trust domain would just be other processes on the same machine, so yes they could, but unless you have some niche audit requirement I don't see why you would.

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: Isn’t this what User Managed Access enabled?

chat.author: RAMPALLI Karthik
chat.text: Check out the PEDIGREE draft.

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text:
Check out the PEDIGREE draft.

This comment should go on Mic

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: I wonder if you could do something with down-scope-able tokens, like you could with Macaroons

chat.author: Pieter Kasselman
chat.text: or Transaction tokens....

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: Aren't transaction tokens bound to a single flow, rather than a series of flows over time?

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: With Transaction tokens the authorization aspects move out of scope and into the Transaction Token Service.

chat.author: Pieter Kasselman
chat.text: Tied to a single transaction - good point

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: But yeah, something of that shape

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: The agent could request a ton-token for each request without “bugging” the user. The TTS could approve based on the pre-authorization.

chat.author: Pamela Dingle
chat.text: I dont know if it matters for historical reasons but the agenda for this session incorrectly shows this presentation as being presented by Paul Carleton (this is the persistent workload delegation session).

chat.author: Justin Richer
chat.text: I mislabled the slides, that'll be fixed.

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: The meeting notes had the right references

chat.author: Justin Richer
chat.text: (it's why I couldn't find them at the beginning)

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: I have it correct from the recently copied agenda - there was just no link for Paul's draft

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: presentation, I mean, he didn't have a draft

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text:
George Fletcher said:

The agent could request a ton-token for each request without “bugging” the user. The TTS could approve based on the pre-authorization.

ton-token?

chat.author: Justin Richer
chat.text: presentation is linked in the notes

chat.author: Aaron Parecki
chat.text: ton = txn autocorrected?

chat.author: Maxwell Gerber
chat.text: What credential does the agent present when it requests the TxToken?

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: What Aaron said!!

chat.author: Pieter Kasselman
chat.text:
What credential does the agent present when it requests the TxToken?

Typically and OAuth Access Token, but other token types are allowed as well.

chat.author: Aaron Parecki
chat.text: I feel like txn token is a big lift compared to what the goal is, since we're talking about a specific problem on a widely deployed existing mechanism

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: That might depend on whether this is a generic pre-authorized use case or a more specific issue.

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text:
George Fletcher said:

The agent could request a ton-token for each request without “bugging” the user. The TTS could approve based on the pre-authorization.

How would the service choose how many txn tokens to request if they don't know how long the user is going to be offline?

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: “ How would the service choose how many txn tokens to request if they don't know how long the user is going to be offline?”

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: As long at it fist the mission of the Agent cf. https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-mcguinness-oauth-mission-00.html

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: Sorry… I failed at chat on my phone:( txt-tokens are per request

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: The reusable part is the pre-authorization mechanism. Which with txn-tokens seems to be out of scope; assuming I’m understanding the proble

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: problem correctly

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: Yeah, it's the pre-auth mechanism and how that's then bound to the txn tokens that seems to be the question.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: feels like confused deputy has become the standard

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: Feels like it starts from the premise of the the Orchestrator shares credential but can it? With WPT the sub agent does not have the key therefore they cannot use the credentials

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: From a cryptographic perspective this is doable in a number of ways.

chat.author: Jeff Lombardo
chat.text: Everything misconfigured lead to doable bad stuf...

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: isn't there some chained SD-JWT CA thing that does something like this?

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: Is this not Authorization?

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: I have been looking at Macaroons recently and they seem like a natural primitive for this, but I don't think they're a well understood (certainly not by me) primitive.

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text: (Or perhaps Biscuits is a more natural fit?)

chat.author: Pamela Dingle
chat.text: Can someone expand SEAT for me?

chat.author: Kathleen Moriarty
chat.text: SEAT is about attested TLS

chat.author: Jonathan Hoyland
chat.text:
Secure Evidence and Attestation Transport
https://datatracker.ietf.org/wg/seat/about/

chat.author: Pamela Dingle
chat.text: Thanks!

chat.author: RAMPALLI Karthik
chat.text: woo hoo

chat.author: RAMPALLI Karthik
chat.text: Congrats.

chat.author: Paul Carleton
chat.text: +1 think there will be a lot of systems building WIF implementations imminently, would love to give them some guidance

chat.author: Nathanael Ritz
chat.text: "A bad WIF smells bad" is great by the way

chat.author: George Fletcher
chat.text: Trust is a multifaceted word. I think we need to be clear about exactly what we mean by “identity federation”. And also how the concepts map to existing federation mechanisms.

chat.author: Jaryn Mervin Sabey
chat.text: Awesome meeting! Related individual I-D on some of the topics today : draft-sabey-succession-receipts , portable, offline-verifiable receipts for authority handoff between workloads/agents, with obligation lineage.

chat.author: Nathanael Ritz
chat.text: Thanks everyone!

chat.author: Dason Tiovino
chat.text: Thanks all!
